commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a CLI compaction branch and regression test so successful native-harness no-op compaction results from the Codex app-server do not throw or trigger context-engine fallback.

PR surface: Source +14, Tests +77. Total +91 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: current main returns a successful Codex-owned non-compacted result for non-manual triggers, and the CLI lifecycle then throws because it only allows fallback for selected failed outcomes. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor real behavior proof or maintainer proof override, not a narrow code repair that ClawSweeper can safely automate.

target.title: fix(agents): don't fail CLI turn when the native harness owns compaction
target.body:
## Summary

Budget/auto-triggered compaction on a Codex-backed session calls the Codex app-server, which **owns automatic compaction** and returns a successful no-op (`{ ok: true, compacted: false, reason: "codex app-server owns automatic compaction" }`, the behavior added in #86772). The host lifecycle `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` in `src/agents/command/cli-compaction.ts` treated any non-compacted native outcome without `fallbackToContextEngine` as a hard failure and threw `CLI native harness compaction failed …`. That fails the agent run (`UNAVAILABLE`) and the session lane never releases — on a large thread the bot shows a "typing" indicator indefinitely and never replies.

This honors a contract the codebase **already** established elsewhere: the auto-reply preflight path skips compaction for Codex runtimes outright (`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts`, "Its harness owns automatic compaction; OpenClaw preflight compaction is only for non-Codex embedded runtimes"). `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` was the one host path that did not honor it and threw instead.

## Fix

Surface an `ownedByNativeHarness` flag on the native outcome when the harness reports a successful no-op (`result.ok === true`), and add a caller branch **before** the throw that skips both the error and the context-engine fallback, letting the turn proceed so the harness compacts itself. Minimal and additive; no refactor. Genuine failures (`ok:false`, thrown, or `undefined` result) and the unsupported / recoverable-binding cases are unchanged.

Note on shape: I extended the existing `NativeHarnessCliCompactionOutcome` (which already uses parallel optional fields) rather than refactoring it into a discriminated union, to keep the fix small. Happy to take the union refactor in a follow-up if maintainers prefer.

## Sibling surfaces (not one-sided)

The other native-harness compaction entry points already tolerate the `ok:true` no-op, so this is the only path that needed fixing:
- `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/compact.queued.ts` — `shouldFallbackAfterHarnessCompaction` only fires on `ok:false`; the `ok:true` no-op is returned directly.
- `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts` — Codex runtimes skip preflight compaction entirely; otherwise only `isDeferredPreflightCompactionReason` throws, which the owned-skip reason is not.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** A successful native-harness compaction no-op (`ok:true, compacted:false`) must not fail the turn or trigger context-engine fallback.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux, Node 22.22.2, exercising the real `runCliTurnCompactionLifecycle` through its dependency seams.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/cli-compaction.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:** 24 passed (2 files). New test `does not throw when the native harness owns automatic compaction (ok:true no-op)` passes. Reverting only the source change makes that test fail with `CLI native harness compaction failed for codex/gpt-5.5: codex app-server owns automatic compaction` (the exact production error).
- **Observed result after fix:** native compactor invoked once, no throw, no context-engine fallback, no store rotation, session entry returned unchanged.
- **What was not tested:** end-to-end channel round-trip (Discord/Telegram) was not re-driven; coverage is at the compaction-lifecycle boundary.

## Verification

`oxfmt --check` clean · `oxlint` clean · `tsgo:test` (core + extensions) exit 0 · diff +14 src / +77 test, 0 deletions.

Complements #87158 (Codex plugin side); coordinates with #87738 (same file, missing-thread `ok:false` case). Related: #85160, #86772, #66263.
